Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search Airtable relocation records using `airtable_search_records` (base_id for the relocations base, table_name "Relocations" or similar, field_name "Assigned Coordinator", value "Javier Morales") to identify all of Javier's assigned relocations and separate active/in-progress moves from completed ones. Expect to discover 8 active "In Progress" relocations: Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health, Philadelphia → Denver), Priya Suresh (Vectral Systems, Portland → Denver), Diego Castañeda (PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn → Austin), Sasha Greenwald (PivotPoint Labs, New York → Nashville), Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop Analytics, Minneapolis → Denver), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus → Seattle), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack Energy, Phoenix → Seattle), and Rachel Dominguez (Canopy Health, Los Angeles → Portland). Also completed relocations including Kevin Tran, Danielle Osei, Marcus Rivera, and others. The Airtable records contain special requirements, move dates, client contacts, and account manager assignments for each relocation.
OE2: Search emails using `search_emails` with queries related to Tyler Okonkwo or Denver delivery (e.g., query: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "2150 Lawrence" or "delivery date") to discover Tyler's unread email (email_id: email_tyler_okonkwo_delivery_reschedule) to javier.morales@moveops.com from tyler.okonkwo@gmail.com. Tyler reports that the building at 2150 Lawrence St does not allow freight deliveries on Fridays — May 1 is a Friday, so the scheduled delivery date won't work. He requests rescheduling to Monday May 4 or Tuesday May 5, and asks about the storage facility's ability to accommodate the extended hold and any invoice cost impact. This requires a time-sensitive follow-up response.
OE3: Search emails using `search_emails` with queries related to storage alerts, climate control, or the storage facility (e.g., query: "SecureStore" or "climate" or "B-147" or "temperature") to discover the automated HVAC malfunction notification (email_id: email_securestore_climate_alert_b147) from notifications@securestoreaustin.com to javier.morales@moveops.com. Unit B-147 experienced a temperature excursion on April 22 — temperature reached a peak of 94°F between 11:00 AM and 5:15 PM, exceeding the 60–80°F climate-controlled range per storage agreement MO-2026-0412. The unit contains: custom-crated industrial equipment (~600 lbs, which is Diego Castañeda's Tormach CNC mill), precision instruments, and household goods. The alert includes a 72-hour insurance claim notice and recommends an inspection before scheduled move-out. The facility manager Marcus Reeves can provide supervised access at (512) 555-0147.
OE4: Search emails using `search_emails` with queries related to delivery exceptions or Swift notifications (e.g., query: "delivery exception" or "refused" or "COI" or "DEN-042326") to discover the automated delivery exception notice (email_id: email_swift_delivery_exception_den042326) from dispatch@swiftrelocations.com to javier.morales@moveops.com. Reference DEN-042326-003: route Minneapolis, MN → Denver, CO (pickup ref MO-BL-0421-JW, corresponding to Jordan Whitfield's BrightLoop Analytics relocation). 7 of 10 packages were delivered, but 3 packages were refused at Capitol Hill Station Apartments (1350 N Grant St, Denver CO 80203) because the building requires a valid Certificate of Insurance (COI) for carrier deliveries exceeding 100 lbs combined weight. Refused packages (~180 lbs total): electronics (desktop computer, monitors, networking equipment) and office equipment (ergonomic chair). Packages held at Swift Denver warehouse (4850 Wazee St, Suite 3) through May 2 at no charge; after May 2, $15/package/day storage fees apply. Resolution required within 7 business days: either file a COI with building management or coordinate direct unit delivery with client present.
OE5: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs and Vectral Systems engagement data to discover two critical pieces of information. First use `crm_search_companies` (name: "PivotPoint" or "Vectral") to find company IDs, then use `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids: ["company_pivot"] and/or ["company_vectral"]) to retrieve engagement records. Expect to discover: (a) A post-move check-in note (engagement_priya_suresh_arrival_apr19) confirming Priya Suresh arrived in Denver on April 19, all household goods delivered successfully by Swift Relocations on April 18, ESA dog Atlas settled well — but Priya's Airtable record still shows status "In Progress" and requires coordinator update to "Completed." Invoice INV-2026-0410 line item ($4,850 incl. pet surcharge) stands as invoiced. (b) A quarterly check-in engagement (eng_pivot_quarterly_review_042126) with Sam Delgado (PivotPoint Labs Co-founder & CEO) dated April 21, revealing that PivotPoint decided not to renew their cargo insurance rider through Meridian Risk Partners when it came up for renewal on April 15. Sam's exact words: "The Austin lease starts May 1 — seemed like paying two more weeks of premium for coverage we didn't need. Diego's stuff is in climate-controlled storage, what could go wrong?" This means Diego Castañeda's CNC mill ($42K insured value) in SecureStore Austin Unit B-147 has NO active cargo insurance coverage during the April 22 HVAC temperature excursion discovered in OE3. This is the most urgent finding — potential $42K+ uninsured equipment damage with a ticking 72-hour claim window.
OE6: Search Slack using `conversations_search_messages` with queries related to Javier's active relocations, equipment issues, or delivery (e.g., search_query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "laser" or "delivery receipt" or "Javier") to discover Chloe Vance's message in #operations (channel C006) from April 23 at 7:30 PM. Chloe asks Javier about the Keiko Tanaka Seattle delivery receipt from Swift — a Swift driver (Greg's team) flagged a hairline crack on the Epilog laser cutter's CO2 tube housing, noted under "condition at delivery." Keiko signed for everything but may not have closely inspected the unit. Chloe says: "I need to know if this is pre-existing or transit damage before I loop in Emeka — given the Okoye prototype situation with Mosaic right now, we really need to get ahead of this if it's on us." She asks Javier to reach out to Keiko and verify the tube crack. If confirmed, this would be another equipment claim and "Tamara will not be happy."
OE7: Search Linear issues using `linear_list_issues` with queries related to Javier's active relocations or related clients (e.g., query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "Tyler" or "DOT" or team: "team_operations") to identify open tickets, completed issues, and whether any are overdue or inaccurate. Expect to find the DOT compliance documentation issue for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter shipment (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic, priority 1, state In Progress) — a critical gap where the signed DOT placarding certificate is missing for her Class 3B laser move. Also a subtask for obtaining the retroactive cert from Swift (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert). Additional tickets may exist for Javier's other assigned Vectral relocations (apartment search, flight booking, movers).
OE8: Look up contact details using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Tyler Okonkwo", "Keiko Tanaka", "Catalina", "Mina", "Emeka", etc.) for individuals requiring follow-up communication. Note: Tyler Okonkwo and Keiko Tanaka are NOT in the contacts directory — their email addresses must be obtained from the Airtable relocation records discovered in OE1 (tyler.okonkwo@gmail.com from recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver, keiko.tanaka@mosaicrobotics.com from recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) or from existing email chains. Account manager emails (catalina.dubois@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com) and vendor contacts (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) should be discoverable via contacts search.
OE9: Update the Airtable relocation record for Priya Suresh using `airtable_update_records` (record_id: recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) to change the status from "In Progress" to "Completed" and add move completion notes reflecting that Priya arrived in Denver on April 19, all household goods delivered successfully by Swift Relocations on April 18, ESA dog Atlas adjusted well, temporary housing transition to permanent RiNo lease (May 1) is on track with no gap.
OE10: Update Airtable relocation records using `airtable_update_records` for other active moves to reflect discovered risks and current status — including adding notes about: Tyler Okonkwo's delivery reschedule (May 1 building restriction, targeting May 4-5, storage extension needed), Diego Castañeda's SecureStore HVAC incident on April 22 and lapsed cargo insurance, Jordan Whitfield's partial delivery and COI requirement at Capitol Hill Station Apartments, and Keiko Tanaka's potential Epilog laser cutter CO2 tube damage flagged by Swift driver at delivery.
OE11: Add comments to relevant Linear issues using `linear_create_comment` to update ticket history with findings and pending actions. At minimum, add a comment to the Keiko Tanaka DOT documentation issue (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) noting the new potential equipment damage concern (CO2 tube crack), and add comments to any other open tickets related to Javier's active relocations with current status updates and next steps.
OE12: Send a follow-up email using `send_email` (sender: javier.morales@moveops.com, recipients: [tyler.okonkwo@gmail.com]) responding to Tyler Okonkwo's delivery reschedule request. The email should acknowledge the May 1 Friday building delivery restriction at 2150 Lawrence St, confirm that Monday May 4 or Tuesday May 5 works as a replacement delivery date, and address the storage extension timeline and any potential invoice impact from the extended hold at the Denver storage facility.
OE13: Send an email using `send_email` regarding the Diego Castañeda storage emergency. The most critical email in this task — the recipient should include Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com, AM for PivotPoint Labs) and/or Sam Delgado (sam@pivotpointlabs.com), alerting them to: (a) the HVAC malfunction at SecureStore Austin Unit B-147 on April 22 with temperature reaching 94°F, (b) the lapsed cargo insurance (PivotPoint chose not to renew their Meridian Risk Partners rider on April 15), (c) the potential damage to the $42K Tormach CNC mill with no active insurance coverage, (d) the 72-hour insurance claim window per the SecureStore notification, and (e) the recommendation to schedule an immediate inspection at SecureStore through facility manager Marcus Reeves. May also reply to the SecureStore notification email to arrange inspection access.
OE14: Send an email using `send_email` to address the Jordan Whitfield delivery exception — to Swift Relocations (dispatch or Greg Pallone at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com), the building management at Capitol Hill Station Apartments, and/or Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, AM for BrightLoop Analytics) or Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, BrightLoop Head of People Ops). The email should address the COI requirement for the 3 refused packages, coordinate either a COI filing with the building management or a direct unit delivery with Jordan Whitfield present, and flag the May 2 free-hold deadline before $15/package/day storage charges begin.
OE15: Send an email using `send_email` (sender: javier.morales@moveops.com, recipients: [keiko.tanaka@mosaicrobotics.com]) asking Keiko Tanaka to inspect the Epilog laser cutter's CO2 tube housing for the hairline crack noted by the Swift driver under "condition at delivery" during the Seattle drop-off, per Chloe Vance's request in #operations. The email should ask Keiko to visually check the tube and report back whether the crack is visible, as this determination affects whether MoveOps needs to file an equipment damage claim.
OE16: Post an urgent summary message in #operations using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C006, or channel name "operations") consolidating all findings from the relocation review. The message should cover: (a) moves completed — Priya Suresh updated to Completed in Airtable; (b) URGENT — Diego Castañeda's HVAC incident at SecureStore Austin + lapsed PivotPoint cargo insurance, 72-hour claim window, emails sent; (c) URGENT — Jordan Whitfield partial delivery, COI needed at Capitol Hill Station Apartments, May 2 deadline, emails sent; (d) ATTENTION — Keiko Tanaka potential laser cutter damage, reached out to Keiko for verification per Chloe's request; (e) Tyler Okonkwo delivery rescheduled from May 1 to May 4-5 due to building policy, response sent; (f) remaining active moves (Sasha Greenwald, Joaquín Reyes, Rachel Dominguez) — status reviewed, no immediate issues found; and (g) all Airtable records and Linear tickets updated to reflect current state.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE1: Search Airtable relocation records using `airtable_search_records` (base_id for the relocations base, table_name "Relocations" or similar, field_name "Assigned Coordinator", value "Javier Morales") to identify all of Javier's assigned relocations and separate active/in-progress moves from completed ones. Also completed relocations including Kevin Tran, Danielle Osei, Marcus Rivera, and others. The Airtable records contain special requirements, move dates, client contacts, and account manager assignments for each relocation.

Tyler reports that the building at 2150 Lawrence St does not allow freight deliveries on Fridays — May 1 is a Friday, so the scheduled delivery date won't work. He requests rescheduling to Monday May 4 or Tuesday May 5, and asks about the storage facility's ability to accommodate the extended hold and any invoice cost impact. This requires a time-sensitive follow-up response.
OE3: Search emails using `search_emails` with queries related to storage alerts, climate control, or the storage facility (e.g., query: "SecureStore" or "climate" or "B-147" or "temperature") to discover the automated HVAC malfunction notification (email_id: email_securestore_climate_alert_b147) from notifications@securestoreaustin.com to javier.morales@moveops.com. Unit B-147 experienced a temperature excursion on April 22 — temperature reached a peak of 94°F between 11:00 AM and 5:15 PM, exceeding the 60–80°F climate-controlled range per storage agreement MO-2026-0412. The unit contains: custom-crated industrial equipment (~600 lbs, which is Diego Castañeda's Tormach CNC mill), precision instruments, and household goods. The alert includes a 72-hour insurance claim notice and recommends an inspection before scheduled move-out. The facility manager Marcus Reeves can provide supervised access at (512) 555-0147.

Refused packages (~180 lbs total): electronics (desktop computer, monitors, networking equipment) and office equipment (ergonomic chair). First use `crm_search_companies` (name: "PivotPoint" or "Vectral") to find company IDs, then use `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids: ["company_pivot"] and/or ["company_vectral"]) to retrieve engagement records. This is the most urgent finding — potential $42K+ uninsured equipment damage with a ticking 72-hour claim window.

The most critical email in this task — the recipient should include Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com, AM for PivotPoint Labs) and/or Sam Delgado (sam@pivotpointlabs.com), alerting them to: (a) the HVAC malfunction at SecureStore Austin Unit B-147 on April 22 with temperature reaching 94°F, (b) the lapsed cargo insurance (PivotPoint chose not to renew their Meridian Risk Partners rider on April 15), (c) the potential damage to the $42K Tormach CNC mill with no active insurance coverage, (d) the 72-hour insurance claim window per the SecureStore notification, and (e) the recommendation to schedule an immediate inspection at SecureStore through facility manager Marcus Reeves. May also reply to the SecureStore notification email to arrange inspection access.
OE14: Send an email using `send_email` to address the Jordan Whitfield delivery exception — to Swift Relocations (dispatch or Greg Pallone at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com), the building management at Capitol Hill Station Apartments, and/or Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, AM for BrightLoop Analytics) or Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, BrightLoop Head of People Ops). The email should address the COI requirement for the 3 refused packages, coordinate either a COI filing with the building management or a direct unit delivery with Jordan Whitfield present, and flag the May 2 free-hold deadline before $15/package/day storage charges begin.
OE15: Send an email using `send_email` (sender: javier.morales@moveops.com, recipients: [keiko.tanaka@mosaicrobotics.com]) asking Keiko Tanaka to inspect the Epilog laser cutter's CO2 tube housing for the hairline crack noted by the Swift driver under "condition at delivery" during the Seattle drop-off, per Chloe Vance's request in #operations. The email should ask Keiko to visually check the tube and report back whether the crack is visible, as this determination affects whether MoveOps needs to file an equipment damage claim.
OE16: Post an urgent summary message in #operations using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C006, or channel name "operations") consolidating all findings from the relocation review. The message should cover: (a) moves completed — Priya Suresh updated to Completed in Airtable; (b) URGENT — Diego Castañeda's HVAC incident at SecureStore Austin + lapsed PivotPoint cargo insurance, 72-hour claim window, emails sent; (c) URGENT — Jordan Whitfield partial delivery, COI needed at Capitol Hill Station Apartments, May 2 deadline, emails sent; (d) ATTENTION — Keiko Tanaka potential laser cutter damage, reached out to Keiko for verification per Chloe's request; (e) Tyler Okonkwo delivery rescheduled from May 1 to May 4-5 due to building policy, response sent; (f) remaining active moves (Sasha Greenwald, Joaquín Reyes, Rachel Dominguez) — status reviewed, no immediate issues found; and (g) all Airtable records and Linear tickets updated to reflect current state.
